pairs.question: Who won the 1986 U.S. Open, and what was notable about his victory?
pairs.answer: Raymond Floyd won the 1986 U.S. Open with a two-stroke lead over runners-up Chip Beck and Lanny Wadkins. His victory was notable as it was his fourth and final major championship, his only U.S. Open title, and he became the oldest winner of the U.S. Open at 43 years and 284 days, surpassing Ted Ray's 1920 record.

pairs.question: What were the weather conditions like during the opening round of the 1986 U.S. Open?
pairs.answer: The opening round on June 12, 1986, featured high winds with occasional heavy rain. The best score of the round was Bob Tway's even-par 70, with Greg Norman finishing one stroke behind.

pairs.question: How did Greg Norman's performance in the 1986 U.S. Open compare to his performance in other majors that year?
pairs.answer: Greg Norman led after 36 holes of the U.S. Open and after 54 holes in each of the four major championships in 1986. However, he won only once, at The Open Championship, and finished twelfth in the U.S. Open after shooting a 75 in the final round.

pairs.question: What was Raymond Floyd's final round score in the 1986 U.S. Open, and how did it impact his position?
pairs.answer: Raymond Floyd shot a bogey-free 66 in the final round, which included birdies at holes 11, 13, and 16. This round allowed him to separate himself from a nine-way tie for the lead, ultimately winning by two strokes over Chip Beck and Lanny Wadkins.

pairs.question: What records did Raymond Floyd break or set with his 1986 U.S. Open victory?
pairs.answer: With his 1986 U.S. Open win, Raymond Floyd became the oldest winner of the U.S. Open at 43 years and 284 days, breaking Ted Ray's 1920 record. He also tied Julius Boros for the oldest winner of a modern major until Phil Mickelson surpassed the record in 2021.

pairs.question: How many U.S. Opens had Raymond Floyd played in prior to his 1986 victory, and what were his best previous finishes?
pairs.answer: Prior to 1986, Raymond Floyd had played in 21 U.S. Opens. His best previous finish was a tie for sixth place in 1965, and his only other top-ten finish was an eighth-place finish in 1971.

pairs.question: Which players tied the course record at the 1986 U.S. Open, and when was it set?
pairs.answer: Chip Beck and Lanny Wadkins tied the course record with scores of 65 in the final round. The course record was originally set earlier in the day by Mark Calcavecchia, who also shot 65.
